(b) \$0.50 per kilometre\\
plus

 The total cost of a journey of $100\,\text{km}$ is \$98.
\newline

\newline

Driver 1  km

Driver 3  km

qtype: Speed-time graph interpretation, acceleration, deceleration, average speed calculation
tex:
The diagram shows the speed--time graph for the first 180 seconds of a train journey.
\newline

(a) Find the acceleration, in m/s$^2$, of the train during the first 50 seconds.
\newline

(b) After 180 seconds, the train decelerates at a constant rate of $1944\,\text{km/h}^2$.
\newline

Show that the train decelerates for 60 seconds until it stops.
\newline

(c) Complete the speed--time graph.
\newline

(d) Calculate the average speed of the train for the whole journey.
\newline

content.content:
Find the acceleration, in m/s$^2$, of the train during the first 50 seconds.
\newline

After 180 seconds, the train decelerates at a constant rate of $1944\,\text{km/h}^2$.
\newline

Show that the train decelerates for 60 seconds until it stops.
\newline

Complete the speed--time graph.
\newline

Calculate the average speed of the train for the whole journey.
\newline

stem.content:
The diagram shows the speed--time graph for the first 180 seconds of a train journey.
\newline

tex:
(a) 

The diagram shows a solid cone and a solid hemisphere.
\newline
\\
The cone has radius $2.4\,\text{cm}$ and slant height $6.3\,\text{cm}$.
\newline
\\
The hemisphere has radius $R\,\text{cm}$.
\newline
\\
The **total** surface area of the cone is equal to the **total** surface area of the hemisphere.
\newline

Calculate the value of $R$.
\newline

[The curved surface area, $A$, of a cone with radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi rl$.
\newline
]\\
{[The curved surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4\pi r^2$.
\newline
]}

(b) 

The diagram shows a solid cone with radius $7.6\,\text{cm}$ and height $16\,\text{cm}$.
\newline
\\
A cut is made parallel to the base of the cone and the top section is removed.
\newline
\\
The remaining solid has height $12\,\text{cm}$, as shown in the diagram.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the remaining solid.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.
\newline
]

content.content:
The diagram shows a solid cone and a solid hemisphere.
\newline
\\
The cone has radius $2.4\,\text{cm}$ and slant height $6.3\,\text{cm}$.
\newline
\\
The hemisphere has radius $R\,\text{cm}$.
\newline
\\
The \textbf{total} surface area of the cone is equal to the \textbf{total} surface area of the hemisphere.
\newline

Calculate the value of $R$.
\newline

[The curved surface area, $A$, of a cone with radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi rl$.
\newline
]\\
{[The curved surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4\pi r^2$.
\newline
]}

The diagram shows a solid cone with radius $7.6\,\text{cm}$ and height $16\,\text{cm}$.
\newline
\\
A cut is made parallel to the base of the cone and the top section is removed.
\newline
\\
The remaining solid has height $12\,\text{cm}$, as shown in the diagram.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the remaining solid.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.
\newline
]

qtype: Currency conversion, rounding, reverse fractions, compound interest (money context)
tex:
(a) The exchange rate is 1 euro $=$ \$1.142.
\newline

(i)  Johann changes \$500 into euros.
\newline

Calculate the number of euros Johann receives.
\newline
\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest euro.
\newline

 Johann buys a computer for \$329.
\newline
\\
The same computer costs 275 euros.
\newline

Calculate the difference in cost in dollars.
\newline

(b) Lucy spends $\dfrac{3}{8}$ of the money she has saved this month on a book that costs \$5.25.
\newline

Calculate how much money Lucy has saved this month.
\newline

(c) Kamal invests \$6130 at a rate of $r$\% per year compound interest.
\newline
\\
The value of his investment at the end of 5 years is \$6669.
\newline

Calculate the value of $r$.
\newline

content.content:
The exchange rate is 1 euro $=$ \$1.142.
\newline

Johann changes \$500 into euros.
\newline

Calculate the number of euros Johann receives.
\newline
\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest euro.
\newline

Johann buys a computer for \$329.
\newline
\\
The same computer costs 275 euros.
\newline

Calculate the difference in cost in dollars.
\newline

Lucy spends $\dfrac{3}{8}$ of the money she has saved this month on a book that costs \$5.25.
\newline

Calculate how much money Lucy has saved this month.
\newline

Kamal invests \$6130 at a rate of $r$\% per year compound interest.
\newline
\\
The value of his investment at the end of 5 years is \$6669.
\newline

Calculate the value of $r$.
\newline

qtype: Venn diagrams (2 and 3 sets), set notation, interpretation, shading
tex:
(a) In the Venn diagram, shade the region $P' \cup Q$.
\newline

(b) There are 50 students in a group.
\newline
\\
34 have a mobile phone ($M$).
\newline
\\
39 have a computer ($C$).
\newline
\\
5 have no mobile phone and no computer.
\newline

Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.
\newline

(c) The Venn diagram shows the number of students in a group of 30 who have brothers ($B$), sisters ($S$) or cousins ($C$).
\newline

(i)  Write down the number of students who have brothers.
\newline

 Write down the number of students who have cousins but do not have sisters.
\newline

 Find $\text{n}(B \cup S \cup C)'$.
\newline

 Use set notation to describe the set of students who have both cousins and sisters but do not have brothers.
\newline

 One student is picked at random from the 30 students.
\newline

Find the probability that this student has cousins.
\newline

 Two students are picked at random from the students who have cousins.
\newline

Calculate the probability that both these students have brothers.
\newline

 One student is picked at random from the 30 students.
\newline

Event $A$   This student has sisters.
\newline
\\
Event $B$   This student has cousins but does not have brothers.
\newline

Explain why event $A$ and event $B$ are equally likely.
\newline

content.content:
In the Venn diagram, shade the region $P' \cup Q$.
\newline

There are 50 students in a group.
\newline
\\
34 have a mobile phone ($M$).
\newline
\\
39 have a computer ($C$).
\newline
\\
5 have no mobile phone and no computer.
\newline

Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.
\newline

The Venn diagram shows the number of students in a group of 30 who have brothers ($B$), sisters ($S$) or cousins ($C$).
\newline

Write down the number of students who have brothers.
\newline

Write down the number of students who have cousins but do not have sisters.
\newline

Find $\text{n}(B \cup S \cup C)'$.
\newline

Use set notation to describe the set of students who have both cousins and sisters but do not have brothers.
\newline

One student is picked at random from the 30 students.
\newline

Find the probability that this student has cousins.
\newline

Two students are picked at random from the students who have cousins.
\newline

Calculate the probability that both these students have brothers.
\newline

One student is picked at random from the 30 students.
\newline

Event $A$ \quad This student has sisters.
\newline
\\
Event $B$ \quad This student has cousins but does not have brothers.
\newline

Explain why event $A$ and event $B$ are equally likely.
\newline

qtype: simplification and addition of algebraic fractions, differentiation, and finding stationary points
tex:
(a) Simplify.
\newline

\[\frac{x^2 - 25}{x^2 - x - 20}\]

(b) Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

\[\frac{x + 5}{x} + \frac{x + 8}{x - 1}\]

(c) A curve has equation $y = 2x^3 - 4x^2 + 6$.
\newline

(i)  Find $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}y}{\mathrm{d}x}$, the derived function of $y$.
\newline

 Calculate the gradient of the curve $y = 2x^3 - 4x^2 + 6$ at $x = 4$.
\newline

 Find the coordinates of the two stationary points on the curve.
\newline

(, ) and (, )

content.content:
Simplify.
\newline

\[\frac{x^2 - 25}{x^2 - x - 20}\]

Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

\[\frac{x + 5}{x} + \frac{x + 8}{x - 1}\]

A curve has equation $y = 2x^3 - 4x^2 + 6$.
\newline

Find $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}y}{\mathrm{d}x}$, the derived function of $y$.
\newline

Calculate the gradient of the curve $y = 2x^3 - 4x^2 + 6$ at $x = 4$.
\newline

Find the coordinates of the two stationary points on the curve.
\newline

(\makebox[1.5cm]{\dotfill}, \makebox[1.5cm]{\dotfill}) and (\makebox[1.5cm]{\dotfill}, \makebox[1.5cm]{\dotfill})

\newline

Explain, giving reasons, why triangle $PQN$ is congruent to triangle $SRN$.
\newline

\newline

$x = $    or   $x = $

\newline

(e) Find $\text{hf}(x) + \text{g}(x)$.
\newline
\\
Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline
